title: Visual + Ex Range
subtitle: Pressing {key::} from Visual auto-fills the range.
summary: When you press : while in *visual mode*, Vim drops you to the command line with `:'<,'>` already typed — the range covering the selection. Just type your command after.

blocks.text: *Visual mode* and Ex commands plug right into each other. Press {key::} from any Visual selection and Vim pre-types the range.

blocks.title: What '< and '> Are
blocks.text:
{key:'}{key:<} and {key:'}{key:>} are *special marks* — they always point at the **first** and **last** line of the **most recent visual selection**. They persist until you make another selection.

This means:

  • Even after exiting Visual, you can do `:'<,'>s/foo/bar/g` from *normal mode* and operate on the last selection.
  • You can move elsewhere, do other work, then come back with {key:g}{key:v} or use `'<` and `'>` as marks in motions ({key:`}{key:<}, {key:`}{key:>}).
Block Visual + {key::} also gets `:'<,'>` — but most Ex commands are line-oriented and don't honor the column boundaries.
